tierBlurbs.Primary: Used frequently as the base colors for establishing the brand. Common across products, docs, and marketing.
tierBlurbs.Secondary: Generally used as accent colors and for semantic purposes throughout products, docs, and marketing.
tierBlurbs.Tertiary: Reserved for generated content — syntax highlighting, charts with many data points. Use only when necessary.

scales.name: gray
scales.desc: The foundation: text, borders, dividers, surfaces. Every other scale leans on these steps to set hierarchy.

scales.name: jade
scales.desc: Cypress green. Reserve for healthy system state — passing tests, positive deltas, successful deploys. Never generic confirmation.

scales.name: teal
scales.desc: Cypress teal. Brand surfaces, primary marketing, and the hue users associate first with the product.

scales.name: indigo
scales.desc: Links, focus rings, and the places attention should land first. The accent that signals interactivity.

scales.name: purple
scales.desc: Used for accents, occasional highlights, and complement to teal. Pairs cleanly with the primary scales without competing.

scales.name: red
scales.desc: Destructive actions, failed tests, errors, blockers. Used sparingly so it keeps its weight where it matters.

scales.name: orange
scales.desc: Flaky tests, pending states, rate limits, degraded behavior. Warmer than red — attention without alarm.

scales.name: fuchsia
scales.desc: Reserved for generated content — syntax highlighting, chart series, data visualizations with many points.

scales.name: green
scales.desc: Reserved for generated content. A yellow-green for chart series and code highlights — distinct from jade.

scales.name: magenta
scales.desc: Reserved for generated content — additional chart series and syntax highlighting tones.
